intro: Calceolaria ponapensis, also known as Ponape bells, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific island of Ponape. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain, swelling, and the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Calceolaria ponapensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calceolaria ponapensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
